divergence: The Black Death targeted only landlords
story_angle: human-interest profile of the one person unaffected by the great event and deeply annoyed about it
headline: Selective Plague Spares Only Gurn Hollow: Landlord-Free Zone Becomes Existential Nightmare
deck: With no one left to bicker over roof repairs, Gurn strives to survive a landscape of abandoned ledgers and ill-timed eviction notices.
article:
Gurn Hollow, the solitary survivor of the recent pestilential upheaval targeting landlords, now finds himself ensconced beneath a mountain of crumbling ledgers and muttering about the unfortunate timing of universal extermination. Once a bustling center of avarice, Gurn's realm has devolved into a desolate tribute to bureaucracy, where every rusted nail in the hollowed structures is counted as a missed opportunity for rent.

Legions of vacant units tease Gurn with the promise of survival as he tallies back-rent in the plague-cart wheel ruts that mar the once-proud cobblestones. "It’s a labor of love, or something like it," he stated, while tightly clutching a quill that has seen far more ink than any tenant has seen in days. With no landlords to demand payment, he busies himself posting 'no subletting' signs on wind-scoured foundations, likely to be ignored by the event horizon of tenant apathy.

Amidst the dust-laden silence, Gurn has discovered the grim joy of his survival ledger, documenting three extra days devoid of a landlord’s incessant curses. "It’s not as liberating as you might think," he mused, his voice echoing through the hollow. "All that’s left is a lot of work and no one to shake down for a roof repair. I even had to build my own chimney to keep the rain out. Now I’m just a glorified handyman in a landscape devoid of tenants."

In stark contrast to the bustling past, the deserted streets mimic a classic comedy of errors. "I used to be the villain in this tale," Gurn lamented, shaking his head. "Now I’m just the fool left to figure out how to patch a roof without a tenant to complain about the leaks. What an unsatisfactory turn of events!" Echoes of former tenants taunt him with memories but provide no solutions, proving that even a plague has its limits when it comes to the absurdity of ownership.

As Gurn Hollow grapples with his newfound autonomy, all he can do is contemplate the unshakeable irony of existence amidst vacant homes and eviction notices. The lack of landlords is a welcome relief, yet it has replaced familiar oppression with the haunting weight of unwanted survival labor. In a world where landlords are dust, one man must wrestle with a heavy heart—and an even heavier leaky roof.
author: B. A. Renter, Bureau Chief of Uninhabitable Locations

weather.condition: Sunny with a chance of landlords

oped.title: Landlords Were the Glue: Why Gurn Hollow’s Freedom Is a Self-Inflicted Wound
oped.author: Sir Reginald P. Fussbudget, Emeritus Chair of Tenant-Shaming Studies
oped.body: The selective plague that spared only Gurn Hollow has exposed a truth the hatless rabble refuse to face: without landlords, civilization devolves into unpaid leaks and existential spreadsheets. Residents now wander abandoned ledgers like lost sheep, unable to bicker productively over rent hikes or moldy ceilings. I propose the only rational remedy—mandatory resurrection of at least one landlord per block via dubious necromancy—to restore order and the comforting ritual of eviction notices. Blaming the victims’ lack of proper headwear during the original outbreak is, of course, secondary but undeniable. Personally, I miss the days when a stern letter could resolve disputes; now I must argue with myself over phantom repairs, which is frankly beneath my station. Let us reinstate the natural hierarchy before everyone starts thinking they own their own roofs.

classifieds.text: Gurn Hollow collective seeks surrogate landlord to bicker daily over nonexistent roof repairs and phantom rent hikes; must furnish own ledgers and eviction stamps. Apply at the abandoned property office before the notices self-evict.

Entire row of vacant tenements for barter, complete with stacks of unpaid ledgers and half-written eviction notices; no actual landlords included. Inquire at Gurn Hollow's central ruin before the structures repossess themselves.

Found: bundle of pre-plague eviction notices fluttering near the hollow; owner may claim them if they can still collect a security deposit in irradiated scrip. Otherwise they become tomorrow's kindling.

Public notice from the last tenant of 17 Ledger Row: we apologize for every ignored maintenance request we once lodged; now that the landlord is gone, the leaks have unionized and demand their own rent.

panels.caption: A yellowed eviction notice flaps on a crumbling door: 'Pay rent or join the landlords.'
Survivor shouts at the sky: 'Who fixes the roof when every owner is dust?'
A fresh notice slides under the door: 'Evicted for lack of landlord to blame.'

joke.setup: In the irradiated husk of Gurn Hollow, where the selective plague left every tenant breathing but every landlord six feet under,
joke.punchline: the survivors finally learned the true cost of freedom: no one left to evict them from their own rotting despair.
joke.text: In the irradiated husk of Gurn Hollow, where the selective plague left every tenant breathing but every landlord six feet under, the survivors finally learned the true cost of freedom: no one left to evict them from their own rotting despair.

sponsor_ads.headline: Gurn Hollow Deed Forge
sponsor_ads.body: Stamp your own property claims on the ruins of vanished landlords before the existential rot sets in. Kits include plague-etched seals and a free dose of simulated scarcity.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Warning: May induce phantom rent anxiety.

sponsor_ads.headline: Selective Scourge Memorial Plots
sponsor_ads.body: Bury your former landlord's ledger in consecrated wasteland soil while stocks last. Each plot guarantees one square meter of landlord-free peace, now with added nightmare ambience.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not responsible for spontaneous property hauntings.

sponsor_ads.headline: Tenant Liberation Stamps
sponsor_ads.body: Official-looking seals that let you declare any husk a sovereign holding in the spared zone. Expires when the collective dread catches up.

sponsor_ads.headline: Nightmare Containment Tarps
sponsor_ads.body: Drape your ex-landlord's gutted manse to muffle the howls of unregulated freedom. Double-layered against the 3387 edition of Black Death irony.
sponsor_ads.tagline: One size fits all abandoned fiefdoms.

real_world_echo: Injury ends Williams' Wimbledon comeback - the selective plague acts as the 'injury' that ends the tenant-landlord contest, stranding the unaffected player with no opponents and forced into pointless action
